chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْإِرَادَةِ أَنَّهَا مِنْ صِفَاتِ الْفِعْلِ وَ سَائِرِ صِفَاتِ الْفِعْلِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – The Intention (Will), it is from the Attributes of the deed, and the rest of the Attributes of the deed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/22)

translations.fr: La Volonté est une chose originée.
translations.zh: 意志是被创造的（后起的）。

narrators.ambiguity_note: Collective anonymous group of al-Kulayni's immediate transmitters

narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi (d. 274/887), a prolific Shia hadith transmitter and author of al-Mahasin
narrators.name_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi

narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi, father of Ahmad, a Shia hadith transmitter
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr al-Azdi (d. 217/832), one of the most prominent and reliable Shia transmitters, known for not narrating from untrustworthy sources
Umar ibn Udhayyna, a trusted Shia jurist and transmitter from the time of Imam al-Sadiq (as)
Muhammad ibn Muslim al-Thaqafi al-Kufi (d. 150/767), one of the most prominent companions of Imam al-Baqir (as) and Imam al-Sadiq (as), narrator of 30,000+ hadiths
Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad al-Sadiq (as), sixth Imam of the Twelver Shia (83-148 AH / 702-765 CE)

bn.الْمَشِيئَةُ: আল-মাশিয়্যাহ: আল্লাহর ইচ্ছা বা অভিপ্রায়; ইসলামি কালামের মূল পরিভাষা
bn.مُحْدَثَةٌ: মুহদাসাহ: উদ্ভূত, অনন্তকালীন নয়; যার শুরু আছে

de.الْمَشِيئَةُ: al-mashi'ah: der Wille oder das Wollen Gottes; Schlüsselbegriff in der islamischen Theologie (Kalam)
de.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdathah: entstanden, nicht ewig, zeitlich begrenzt

en.الْمَشِيئَةُ: al-mashi'ah: the Will or Volition of God; a key term in Islamic theology (kalam) distinguishing God's active will from His eternal essence
en.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdathah: originated, brought into being, non-eternal; the theological term for that which had a beginning in time

es.الْمَشِيئَةُ: al-mashi'ah: la Voluntad o Querer de Dios; término central en la teología islámica (kalám)
es.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdathah: originada, no eterna, surgida en el tiempo

fa.الْمَشِيئَةُ: مشیئت: اراده یا خواست خداوند؛ اصطلاح مهم در علم کلام
fa.مُحْدَثَةٌ: حادث: پدیدآمده، دارای آغاز، غیر ازلی

fr.الْمَشِيئَةُ: al-mashi'ah : la Volonté ou le Vouloir de Dieu ; terme central en théologie islamique (kalâm)
fr.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdathah : originée, non éternelle, ayant un commencement

id.الْمَشِيئَةُ: al-mashi'ah: Kehendak atau Keinginan Allah; istilah utama dalam teologi Islam
id.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdathah: diciptakan, baru ada, tidak azali

ru.الْمَشِيئَةُ: аль-маши'а: Воля или Желание Бога; ключевой термин в исламском богословии (калам)
ru.مُحْدَثَةٌ: мухдаса: возникшая, не вечная, имеющая начало

tr.الْمَشِيئَةُ: el-meşîe: Allah'ın iradesi veya dilemesi; kelâm ilminde temel kavram
tr.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdese: sonradan var edilmiş, ezelî olmayan; hudûs kavramı

ur.الْمَشِيئَةُ: مشیئت: اللہ کا ارادہ یا خواہش؛ علمِ کلام کی اہم اصطلاح
ur.مُحْدَثَةٌ: حادث: وجود میں آنے والا، ابتدا رکھنے والا؛ جو ازلی نہ ہو

zh.الْمَشِيئَةُ: al-mashi'ah：真主的意志或意愿；伊斯兰神学（卡拉姆）中的核心术语
zh.مُحْدَثَةٌ: muhdathah：后起的、非永恒的、有起始的

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম আল-সাদিক আল্লাহর ইচ্ছার স্বভাব সম্পর্কে — এটি অনন্তকালীন নাকি সৃষ্ট — কী বলেছেন?
seo_questions.de: Was sagte Imam al-Sadiq über die Natur des göttlichen Willens — ist er ewig oder erschaffen?
seo_questions.en: What did Imam al-Sadiq say about the nature of God's Will — is it eternal or created?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué dijo el Imam al-Sadiq sobre la naturaleza de la Voluntad Divina — es eterna o creada?
seo_questions.fa: امام صادق علیه‌السلام درباره ماهیت مشیئت الهی — ازلی یا مخلوق — چه فرمودند؟
seo_questions.fr: Qu'a dit l'Imam al-Sadiq sur la nature de la Volonté divine — est-elle éternelle ou créée ?
seo_questions.id: Apa yang dikatakan Imam al-Sadiq tentang sifat Kehendak Ilahi — apakah azali atau diciptakan?
seo_questions.ru: Что сказал Имам ас-Садик о природе Божественной Воли — является ли она вечной или сотворённой?
seo_questions.tr: İmam Sadık Allah'ın iradesinin ezeli mi yoksa yaratılmış mı olduğu hakkında ne demiştir?
seo_questions.ur: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے اللہ کی مشیئت کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا — کیا یہ ازلی ہے یا مخلوق؟
seo_questions.zh: 萨迪格伊玛目关于神圣意志的性质说了什么——它是永恒的还是被创造的？

summaries.bn: ইমাম আল-সাদিক (আ.) এই সংক্ষিপ্ত কিন্তু গভীর কালামি বক্তব্যে ঘোষণা করেছেন যে আল্লাহর ইচ্ছা (আল-মাশিয়্যাহ) হলো 'মুহদাসাহ' — অর্থাৎ এটি আল্লাহর সাথে সহ-অনন্তকালীন নয়, বরং নিজেই একটি সৃষ্ট বাস্তবতা। শিয়া কালামে এটি একটি কেন্দ্রীয় অবস্থান যা সত্তার গুণ এবং কর্মের গুণের মধ্যে পার্থক্য করে।
summaries.de: Imam al-Sadiq (Friede sei mit ihm) erklärte in dieser knappen, aber tiefgründigen theologischen Aussage, dass der göttliche Wille (al-mashi'ah) 'muhdathah' ist — also entstanden, nicht ko-ewig mit Gott, sondern eine erschaffene Wirklichkeit. Dies ist eine zentrale Position der schiitischen Kalam-Theologie, die Wesens- von Handlungsattributen unterscheidet.
summaries.en: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) declared in this brief but profound theological statement that the Divine Will (al-mashi'ah) is 'originated' (muhdathah) — meaning it is not co-eternal with God but is itself a created reality. This was a central position in early Shia kalam (speculative theology), distinguishing the Will from God's eternal essence. The context of this chapter — which deals with whether the Will is an attribute of the act (sifat al-fi'l) rather than an attribute of the essence (sifat al-dhat) — places this hadith within the broader Shia theological framework that safeguards Divine transcendence.
summaries.es: El Imam al-Sadiq (la paz sea con él) declaró en esta breve pero profunda afirmación teológica que la Voluntad Divina (al-mashi'ah) es 'muhdathah' — originada, es decir, no co-eterna con Dios sino una realidad creada. Esta es una posición central en el kalám chiita que distingue los atributos de la esencia de los atributos del acto divino.
summaries.fa: امام جعفر صادق علیه‌السلام در این گفته کوتاه اما عمیق کلامی بیان داشتند که مشیئت الهی (اراده) «حادث» است — یعنی ازلی نیست بلکه خود مخلوق است. این موضع محوری در کلام شیعی است که مشیئت را از ذات ازلی خداوند جدا می‌کند و آن را از جمله «صفات فعل» می‌شمارد نه «صفات ذات».
summaries.fr: L'Imam al-Sadiq (que la paix soit sur lui) a déclaré dans cette brève mais profonde affirmation théologique que la Volonté divine (al-mashi'ah) est 'muhdathah' — originée, c'est-à-dire non co-éternelle avec Dieu, mais une réalité créée. C'est une position centrale du kalâm chiite qui distingue les attributs de l'essence divine des attributs de l'acte.
summaries.id: Imam al-Sadiq (as) menyatakan dalam pernyataan teologis singkat namun mendalam ini bahwa Kehendak Ilahi (al-mashi'ah) adalah 'muhdathah' — artinya bukan bersifat azali bersama Allah, melainkan merupakan realitas yang diciptakan. Ini adalah posisi sentral dalam kalam Syiah, yang membedakan antara sifat dzat dan sifat fi'il.
summaries.ru: Имам ас-Садик (мир ему) заявил в этом кратком, но глубоком богословском высказывании, что Божественная Воля (аль-маши'а) является «мухдасой» — возникшей, то есть не совечной Богу, а сотворённой реальностью. Это центральная позиция шиитского калама, отличающая атрибуты сущности от атрибутов действия.
summaries.tr: İmam Sadık (a.s.) bu kısa fakat derin kelâmî ifadede Allah'ın iradesinin (el-meşîe) 'sonradan var edilmiş' (muhdese) olduğunu, yani Allah'ın ezelî zâtıyla eş-ezelî olmadığını beyan etmiştir. Bu, Şiî kelâmının önemli bir tutumudur; zâtî sıfatlar ile fiilî sıfatlar arasındaki ayrımı korur.
summaries.ur: امام جعفر صادق علیہ السلام نے اس مختصر لیکن گہرے کلامی بیان میں فرمایا کہ اللہ کی مشیئت (ارادہ) حادث ہے یعنی یہ ذاتِ الٰہی کی طرح ازلی نہیں بلکہ مخلوق ہے۔ یہ موقف شیعہ کلام میں ذاتی صفات اور فعلی صفات کے درمیان فرق کرتا ہے۔ باب کا سیاق یہ ہے کہ مشیئت 'صفتِ فعل' ہے نہ کہ 'صفتِ ذات'۔
summaries.zh: 萨迪格伊玛目（愿主赐他平安）在这句简短而深刻的神学陈述中宣称，神圣意志（al-mashi'ah）是"穆赫达萨"——即后起的，不与真主同在于永恒，而是一个被创造的实在。这是什叶派卡拉姆神学的核心立场，区分了本体属性与行为属性。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/22)

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Azina, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The Desire is a creation’.
en.sarwar: 7. A group of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ibn ’Udhaynah from Muhammad ibn Muslim from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said, “The will is created.” Summery Statements on the Attributes of His self Sifat al-Dhat and the Attributes of His Actions Sifat al-Fi‘1 For every two attributes which you attribute to Allah (e.g. The Creator, The Sustainer), of which each has its own separate being, (i.e. The Creator achieves its being, as an attribute of Allah’ through something which Allah creates, and The Sustainer achieves its being, as an attribute of Allah, through a created being which He sustains), these attributes are attributes of (His) action. (Because if two attributes are separate in being, obviously each of them differs in their being with the thing with which they are attributed, and therefore both the attribute and the attributed have their own being. Therefore, in order to distinguish an attribute of action from an attribute of essence, it is always necessary to compare that attribute with another one. If we perceives these two to be independent of each other in being, then both of them are attributes of action). The explanation (and proof) of this sentence is that you prove (establish), in respect of His Being, what He intends to do and what He does not intend, what pleases Him and what displeases Him, what He likes and what He dislikes. If the intention had been the attribute of His essence like the attribute of knowledge and power, His not intending (for something) would have been its contradiction. Similarly, if His liking for something had been the attribute of His essence, His dislike (of something) would have been its contradiction. Do you not see, we do not find in His Being anything which He does not know and anything over which He has no power? Such are the attributes of His essence (Self), which are everlasting. We can never attribute to Allah power and (its opposite) weakness. (Similarly, we can neither attribute to Him knowledge and [its opposite] ignorance and stupidity, nor can we attribute to Him wisdom and 1 its opposite] error. Nor can we attribute to Him honor and [its opposite] disgrace). It is permissible to say, “He (Allah) loves him who obeys Him and hates him who disobeys Him. He favors him who obeys Him and is hostile to him who disobeys Him. And verily, He is pleased and He is also displeased. It is also permissible to say in the invocations: ‘O my Allah, be pleased with me and do not be angry at me. Favor me and do not be hostile to me.’” But it is not permissible to say, “Allah has power to know and has no power not to know. He has power to possess and has no power not to possess. He has power to be powerful and wise, and has no power to be otherwise, i.e. without power and wisdom. He has power to be generous and has no power to be not generous. He has power to be forgiving and has no power to be not forgiving.” It is also not permissible to say, “Allah intended to be the Nourishing, the Everlasting, the Mighty, the Wise, the Possessor, the all-knowing, the all-Powerful.” Since these are the attributes of His essence while intention is among His attributes of action. “Do you not see that it is said; “Allah intended this and did not intend that.” Every Attribute of His essence negates its opposite in respect of Him. He is called: “The Living, the All-knowing, the All-hearing, the All-seeing, the All-mighty, the All-wise, the Rich, the King, the Clement, the Just and the Generous.” The opposite of knowledge is ignorance. The opposite of power is inability. The opposite of life is death. The opposite of honor is humiliation. The opposite of wisdom is error. The opposite of clemency is haste and ignorance. The opposite of justice is oppression and tyranny. (So, by proving any of His essential attributes its opposite is negated.)
